sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 19.9g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. which is exactly what you want from a legume.
sections.subtitleText: 19.9g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. which is exactly what you want from a legume.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 30.2g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.
sections.subtitleText: 30.2g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 36.5g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.
sections.subtitleText: 36.5g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin c at 10% daily value stands out most. that is barely moving the needle.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin c at 10% daily value stands out most. that is barely moving the needle.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: magnesium at 70% daily value, iron at 49% daily value, zinc at 37% daily value stand out most. that gives this food real mineral support.
sections.subtitleText: magnesium at 70% daily value, iron at 49% daily value, zinc at 37% daily value stand out most. that gives this food real mineral support.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: pros first: extreme protein/mineral legume because tests whether legumes can handle a very non-typical high-fat, high-protein member. very low glycemic behaviour because strong category fit. many practical uses because broad food-system relevance.

sections.narration: the drawbacks next: anti-nutrients matter because preparation and form matter a lot. often consumed in processed forms because whole soybeans are not the only real-world form. can be rough on some people because practical downside.

closing.summary: Soybeans has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
